g.text: TLB Hit vs Miss: Memory Access Count
Multi-Level Page Tables: The Problem Gets Worse
Why TLB Hit Rate Dominates Performance
TLB Hit (fast path) | 1-2 cycles | TLB lookup only
TLB Miss + PT in L1 | 5-10 cycles | L1 cache hit for PTE
TLB Miss + PT in L2 | 15-25 cycles | L2 cache hit for PTE
TLB Miss + PT in L3 | 40-60 cycles | L3 cache hit for PTE
TLB Miss + PT in RAM | 100-200 cycles | Full memory walk

div.p: Key Insight: TLB hit cost is constant (1-2 cycles) regardless of page table depth. TLB miss cost scales linearly with levels: 2 → 3 → 5 memory accesses. This is why TLB coverage matters MORE on 64-bit systems with deep page tables.

g.text: Dropping from 99% to 90% hit rate = 5× slower memory access
